risks: Recovery Services Vault is deployed in the source region (eastus2); Microsoft documentation states the vault should be in the target region for cross-regional DR to ensure failover operations remain accessible during a source-region outage
No Site Recovery replication policy or replicated item resources are defined — the plan provisions infrastructure but omits the actual replication configuration linking VMs to the vault
No Network Security Groups (NSGs) defined for any subnets, leaving all three tiers (web, app, db) without network-level access controls
No monitoring or alerting resources (Log Analytics workspace, Azure Monitor alerts) to track replication health or failover readiness
No availability zone configuration for primary VMs — a zonal failure in eastus2 could trigger unnecessary cross-region failover

Target storage account (stor-target-westus2) uses GRS, which is unnecessary overhead; Azure Site Recovery documentation states cache and target storage do not need geo-redundancy since data is already replicated cross-region
No capacity reservations in the target region (westus2) — failover VMs may not find available compute capacity during a regional incident
Automation Account has no runbooks defined for the DNS failback automation described in the user goal
correctionsRecommended: Move Recovery Services Vault (rsv-primary-eastus2) to westus2 and rename appropriately — required for Azure-to-Azure cross-regional replication per Microsoft documentation
Add Site Recovery replication policy resource with RPO threshold, recovery point retention, and app-consistent snapshot frequency
Add Site Recovery replicated item resources for each VM (vm-web-01, vm-app-01, vm-db-01) linking source VM, cache storage account, target VNet, and target storage account
Add NSG resources for each subnet in both primary and secondary VNets with appropriate inbound/outbound rules per tier
Add a Log Analytics workspace and configure diagnostic settings on the Recovery Services Vault, VMs, and Traffic Manager for operational visibility
Add Azure Monitor alert rules for Site Recovery replication health and Traffic Manager endpoint degradation
Change target storage account SKU from Standard_GRS to Standard_LRS — geo-redundancy is unnecessary for Site Recovery replicated data
Define Automation runbooks for post-failover DNS update and failback orchestration scripts
Consider adding availability zone configuration for primary VMs to improve resilience within the source region
Consider adding on-demand capacity reservations in westus2 for the VM SKUs used in the plan
hardDependencies: Recovery Services Vault must be relocated to the target region (westus2) — Azure Site Recovery does not support cross-regional replication when the vault is in the same region as the source VMs
Site Recovery replicated items must be defined to establish the replication link between source VMs, cache storage, target VNet, and target storage account
